components.name: Label
components.description: An accessible form label linked to a control via htmlFor. Supports a required indicator (red asterisk). Automatically dims when the paired control is disabled.

examples.name: Default States
examples.description: Standard label and required label side by side.

examples.name: Disabled States
examples.description: Disabled label with and without required indicator.

props.name: htmlFor
props.description: ID of the associated form control (required for accessibility)

props.name: children
props.description: Label text — include a destructive span for required indicator

bestPractices.do: Always set htmlFor matching the input id — this is required for click-to-focus and screen reader association.
bestPractices.dont:
Use a plain or

as a visual label without htmlFor — screen readers cannot associate it with the input.

bestPractices.do: Place visible labels above or beside every input. Use placeholder only as supplementary hint.
bestPractices.dont: Replace labels with placeholders — placeholders disappear on focus and are not accessible labels.

bestPractices.do: Add a destructive asterisk inside the label text to mark required fields consistently.
bestPractices.dont: Use color alone to communicate 'required' — always pair it with a text symbol like *.

keyboard.action: Move focus to the associated form control

notes: htmlFor must match the id of the associated control — required for all screen readers
Clicking the label programmatically focuses and activates the linked control
role is implicitly 'label' via the native <label> element — no aria-label needed
For required fields, also add the required attribute on the input for form validation

related.name: Input
related.desc: Standard text field — always paired with a Label via htmlFor for accessibility.

related.name: Checkbox
related.desc: Toggle control — label placed inline beside the checkbox using flex items-center.

related.name: Switch
related.desc: Boolean control — same inline layout pattern as Checkbox.
